title: OWASP Foundation Security Standards
description: Web application vulnerability categories (OWASP Top 10), mitigation blueprints, and authentication checklists.
markdown:
# OWASP Web Application Security Reference

The Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP) is a non-profit foundation that works to improve the security of software.

## OWASP Top 10 Vulnerabilities Summary

### 1. A01:2021-Broken Access Control
Users can access resources outside their intended privileges.
- **Prevention**: Enforce denylist-by-default access, use UUIDs/cryptographic keys instead of incremental integers (IDOR defense), and perform server-side privilege checks on every request.

### 2. A03:2021-Injection
Untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query (e.g. SQL Injection, XSS).
- **Prevention**: Use parameterized queries (prepared statements), sanitize inputs, and use Context-Aware Output Encoding.

### 3. A07:2021-Identification and Authentication Failures
Weaknesses in login, password recovery, or session token management.
- **Mitigation Checklist**:
  - Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).
  - Enforce password strength guidelines (minimum 8-10 chars, complexity).
  - Use cryptographically strong session cookies with `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, and `SameSite=Strict` flags.
  - Implement rate limiting on login attempts.

title: Docker & Kubernetes Deployment Guides
description: Containerization configurations, multi-container compose settings, and Kubernetes orchestration architectures.
markdown:
# Docker & Kubernetes Containerization Reference

Containerization allows packaging an application with all of its dependencies into a single deployment artifact.

## Dockerfile Setup
Create a standardized container image for Node.js.
```dockerfile
# Base image
FROM node:18-alpine

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Install dependencies
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production

# Copy application source
COPY . .

# Expose server port and run
EXPOSE 3000
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]
```

## Docker Compose Configuration (`docker-compose.yml`)
Run web servers and databases simultaneously.
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgres://db_user:password@db:5432/my_db
    depends_on:
      - db
  db:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: db_user
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
      POSTGRES_DB: my_db
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

## Kubernetes Pod Configuration (`pod.yaml`)
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: express-app-pod
  labels:
    app: express-web
spec:
  containers:
  - name: web-container
    image: express-app:latest
    ports:
    - containerPort: 3000
```

title: Git, GitHub & Jenkins CI/CD
description: Version control workflows, GitHub Actions CI/CD automation pipelines, and Jenkins servers.
markdown:
# Git & CI/CD Automation Manual

Version control and automation form the core of modern DevOps release pipelines.

## Git Command Reference
- **Clone repository**: `git clone <repo-url>`
- **Create local branch**: `git checkout -b <branch-name>`
- **View changes**: `git status`
- **Stage & commit**: `git add . && git commit -m "commit message"`
- **Push commits**: `git push origin <branch-name>`
- **Rebase main**: `git checkout main && git pull && git checkout - && git rebase main`

## GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)
Automate code testing and linting on every branch push.
```yaml
name: Node CI Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, dev ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: Checkout Code
      uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
    - name: Setup Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-node: 18
        cache: 'npm'
        
    - name: Install Dependencies
      run: npm ci
      
    - name: Run Linter
      run: npm run lint
      
    - name: Run Unit Tests
      run: npm run test
```

title: freeCodeCamp Technical Learning
description: Consolidated guidelines on frontend skills, backend paths, and devops roadmaps from freeCodeCamp.
markdown:
# freeCodeCamp Roadmaps & Skill Guides

freeCodeCamp.org is a free, open-source community learning platform that helps students learn to code through interactive modules and technical tutorials.

## Frontend Engineering Checklist
1. **HTML & CSS**: Flexbox, CSS Grid layouts, semantic markup, and responsive media queries.
2. **JavaScript**: Dynamic DOM events, array utilities, ES6 scopes, and fetch API operations.
3. **Frameworks**: React components, hooks (useState, useEffect), and global context states.
4. **Tooling**: Node, npm package manager, Git/GitHub, and code editors.

## Backend Engineering Checklist
1. **Programming Core**: Object-oriented scripting, asynchronous callbacks, and data structures.
2. **Servers**: Node.js, Express web servers, routing, middleware, CORS, and response headers.
3. **Databases**: Relational PostgreSQL (tables, indices) and NoSQL MongoDB (JSON collections).
4. **Security**: Password crypt hashing (bcrypt), JSON Web Tokens (JWT), and injection sanitization.

title: Smashing Magazine Engineering Deep Dives
description: Deep-dives into modern toolsets, scalability issues, and performance challenges facing developers.
markdown:
# Smashing Magazine Technical Summaries

Smashing Magazine provides design and development articles, coding tips, and challenges for digital creatives and web developers.

## Current Web Application Challenges

### 1. Frontend Performance & Core Web Vitals
- **LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)**: Measures loading performance. Optimize image sizes and prevent render-blocking resources.
- **FID (First Input Delay)**: Measures interactivity. Keep main execution tasks short, split long tasks.
- **CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)**: Measures visual stability. Always define dimension size ratios on images and components.

### 2. Microservice Architectures & Distributed Backend
- **Data consistency**: Managing transactions across multiple microservice databases (Saga Pattern).
- **Service Discovery**: Resolving dynamically changing network IP locations for dockerized backend containers (Consul, Kubernetes DNS).
- **Monitoring & Tracing**: Debugging server execution pipelines across network routes (Jaeger, OpenTelemetry).

title: Real Python Technical References
description: Python syntax primers, decorator mechanics, memory/speed profiling, and coding tools from Real Python.
markdown:
# Real Python Reference & Code Guidelines

Real Python provides high-quality tutorials, reference materials, and video courses for developers learning Python.

## Core Python Features & Syntax

### 1. Decorator Mechanics
Decorators allow modifying the behavior of a function without directly changing its code.
```python
import time

def timer_decorator(func):
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        start = time.time()
        result = func(*args, **kwargs)
        end = time.time()
        print(f"Executed {func.__name__} in {end - start:.4f} seconds")
        return result
    return wrapper

@timer_decorator
def heavy_computation():
    sum(i * i for i in range(1000000))

heavy_computation()
```

### 2. Python Virtual Environments (venv)
Keep project dependencies isolated.
```bash
# Create virtual environment
python -m venv .venv

# Activate venv (Windows)
.venv\Scripts\activate

# Install dependencies from manifest
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## Code Performance Profiling
- **cProfile**: Native Python library to analyze runtime and identify performance bottlenecks.
  ```bash
  python -m cProfile script.py
  ```

title: Career Portals & Job Market Statistics
description: Job scopes, career path roadmaps, and Bureau of Labor Statistics data for software engineering roles.
markdown:
# Software Engineering Career Roadmaps & Statistics

A guide compiling roles, daily operations, and job market metrics for software developers.

## Roles & Responsibilities

| Role | Core Focus | Common Technologies | US Job Growth (BLS) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Frontend Developer** | User Interfaces & Experiences | React, Next.js, HTML/CSS, TypeScript | 16% (Much faster than average) |
| **Backend Developer** | Server logic, Database, API systems | Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, REST APIs | 25% (Much faster than average) |
| **DevOps Engineer** | Automation, Infrastructure & Deployment | Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD | 25% (Much faster than average) |
| **Cybersecurity Expert** | Auditing, Network Protection, Pen Testing | Wireshark, Burp Suite, Zap, Metasploit | 35% (Extremely high demand) |

## A Day in the Life
- **Software Engineers**: Writing modular code, debugging pipeline integrations, reviewing pull requests, participating in agile stand-up meetings, and writing system unit tests.
- **DevOps Engineers**: Provisioning cloud networks, fixing container registry builds, scaling server pods, and optimizing automated delivery scripts.

title: Cybersecurity Pathways (Cyberseek)
description: Vulnerability analysis paths, certification checklists (CISSP, CEH), and workforce heatmaps from Cyberseek.
markdown:
# Cybersecurity Career Pathway Reference

Detailed career information, training metrics, and certification pathways compiled from Cyberseek.org and Cyber Degrees.

## Interactive Career Pathways

### 1. Entry-Level Roles
- **Cybersecurity Specialist / Technician**: Monitor logs, analyze events, configure firewall rules, and patch known server flaws.
- **Incident Responder**: Handle active breaches, perform forensics, isolate compromised server containers, and restore backups.

### 2. Mid-Level Roles
- **Penetration Tester (Ethical Hacker)**: Find vulnerabilities in application APIs and corporate networks before malicious hackers do.
- **Cybersecurity Consultant**: Audit architecture designs, assess compliance with security benchmarks (e.g. ISO 27001, SOC2).

## Core Certification Targets
- **CompTIA Security+**: Entry-level baseline credential covering security concepts and operations.
- **Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH)**: Covers scan strategies, system penetration, threat vectors, and hacking utilities.
- **CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional)**: Advanced architecture-level credential focusing on policy, management, risk, and security governance.
